toast.openChatToRevise: Open the chat to revise a chain.

chat.welcome: MAESTRO builds agent chains for your tasks — not a slow search, but a reproducible pipeline of linked steps. Describe a task to get started. Jump to /artifacts (chains from this chat), /library (saved chains), /evolution (evolve a chain) or /settings. Type /help for all commands.

tooltip.adHoc: Agent chain generation: I design a chain of linked steps for your task. By default I ask before running (run-immediately in /settings). Save, edit (/revise), or reuse the chain only when you decide. Works without Memory configured.
tooltip.production: Agent creation: I build a reproducible agent, save it to Memory under a stable chain_id, seed a baseline run for quality tracking, and (when Platform is wired) kick off evolution. Needs CARE_MEMORY__BASE_URL (and CARE_MEMORY__API_KEY when auth is enabled) — otherwise it falls back to Chat with a warning.

welcomeTail.agent: Agent-creation mode — I'll build a reproducible agent, save it to Memory under a stable chain_id, and seed a baseline run. Evolution (when Platform is wired) starts automatically. The agent ID is printed once it's built so you can reference it later. Back to quick answers: /mode chat.
welcomeTail.chat: You're in agent chain generation mode — I quickly build a chain of linked steps for your task. When the chain is ready, you can run it and review the result, save it to the library, or edit individual steps. Attach a file with @notes.md — its contents are embedded in the task. To re-run a saved chain: /library → Run. Type your task and press Enter. Saved chains can be evolved when labeled data is available — see «More about evolution» or «Working with data» below.

flipHint.agent: Now in Agent-creation mode — built agents are saved to Memory under a stable chain_id, a baseline run is recorded for quality tracking, and (when Platform is wired) evolution kicks off.
flipHint.chat: Now in agent chain generation mode — I build a chain of steps for your task; by default I ask before running (run-immediately in /settings). Save or edit with the Save button and /revise.

prod.datasetTip: Score the most recent chain on its saved dataset
prod.evolveTip: Open evolution for the most recent chain

tour.step1:
Tour 1/5 — Welcome to MAESTRO.
MAESTRO is a chat-first agent ecosystem: describe a task, MAESTRO generates a reasoning chain, runs it, and shows the result inline. Press Enter to continue, Esc to exit.
tour.step2:
Tour 2/5 — Interactive mode.
You're in Interactive right now. Type any task — `summarise the README` or `find every TODO in src/` — and MAESTRO generates a chain, then (by default) asks before running it. The chain is disposable; save it with the Save button if you want to keep it. Switch to run-immediately in /settings.
tour.step3:
Tour 3/5 — Production mode.
Switch with /mode production. Each task becomes a reproducible chain saved to Memory under a stable chain_id, with a baseline dataset entry seeded for quality tracking. Use this when you're building a real agent, not just exploring.
tour.step4:
Tour 4/5 — Library and datasets.
/library lists saved chains — highlight a row and press Run to execute with new inputs. /run <chain_id> opens read-only chain details. /dataset list / add / run / export manage evaluation data for quality tracking.
tour.step5:
Tour 5/5 — Evolution.
/evolution <run_id> shows live status of an evolution run; /evolution watch <run_id> streams events as they happen. That's the tour — press Enter to finish, then type a task to begin or /help for the full command list.
tour.offer: First time here? Type /tour for a 5-step walkthrough.
tour.complete: Tour complete. Type any task to begin, or /help for the full command list.
tour.exited: Tour exited. Type /tour to start over.
tour.busy: Can't start the tour while generation is in flight — press Esc first.

chat.productionTutorial:
First-time Production tutorial — here's what happens on your next message:
  1. The agent chain generator produces a reproducible chain for the task.
  2. The chain is saved to Memory under a stable `chain_id` — copy it with Ctrl+Y / inspect with /run <id>.
  3. MAESTRO runs the chain once on the same task and seeds the first dataset entry (the baseline) so quality is measurable from day one.
  4. If Platform is wired, evolution kicks off automatically; otherwise the step is logged as "Evolution skipped — Platform not configured."
Use /dataset list <chain_id> to view your dataset, /dataset run to score it, /evolution <run_id> for live evolution state. /help shows everything Production mode exposes.

Follow-ups: once a chain is saved, your next plain messages are treated as revision instructions against that chain (e.g. "add structured output in the final step"). The agent chain generator plans a minimal edit, previews the diff, and on confirm saves a new version under the same id. Use /new to drop the revision target and start a fresh chain instead.

stage.chainFinished: Work on this chain is complete — describe a new task in the prompt to continue.
stage.runDeclined: Run skipped — the chain was generated but not executed.

confirm.runGuide:
Chain ready — what next?
• Run now — Run
• Save to library — Save
• Edit steps — Edit
• New task without running — Skip

help.modesBlock:
Interactive  Generate a chain, then (by default) confirm
               before running it — the chain is disposable.
               Save it with the Save button if you want.
               Toggle run-immediately in /settings.
  Production   Generate a reproducible chain, save it to
               Memory under a stable chain_id, seed a
               baseline dataset, and (when Platform is
               wired) kick off evolution.
help.scopeAdHoc: (Interactive mode commands — switch to /mode production for /dataset)
help.evolutionSetup: Open the setup modal to launch a new run
help.evolutionBare: Evolution primer, then the runs dashboard
help.footer:
Anything that isn't a /command is sent to the agent chain generator as a task description.

Keys: Esc interrupt · Ctrl+L clear · Ctrl+↑/↓ history
      Ctrl+Y copy reply · Ctrl+Shift+Y copy all
      Ctrl+\ history sidebar · Alt+T cycle theme
Selection: drag to highlight any text — release auto-copies to the clipboard. Ctrl+C also copies the current selection (Cmd+C on terminals that forward it).

settings.keysHint: Memory + Platform API keys are read from env vars (CARE_MEMORY__API_KEY / CARE_PLATFORM__API_KEY) or your care.toml.

hint.mage: Model + access for the agent chain generator — the engine that turns your task into an agent (a chain of steps). Set what generates and with which key here.
hint.memory: Where MAESTRO stores saved chains and their runs (GigaEvo Memory). Needed for Agent-creation mode.
hint.platform: Agent evolution (GigaEvo Platform). Mutation = the engine proposing variants; Validation = the judge scoring them. Both are written into the local Platform's llm_models.yml.
hint.search: Web-search engine — shared by the agent chain generator and MAESTRO's built-in web_search tool.
hint.theme: MAESTRO's appearance and interface language. The interface language doesn't change the agent's answer language.

dataIntro.title: Working with data in MAESTRO
dataIntro.whatBody:
Three ways to give MAESTRO your source material:
• @file in chat — embed a document in a new task
• Library → Run — re-run a saved chain with a task and context files
• /dataset — scored examples for evaluation and evolution
dataIntro.whyBody: Chains need your text or documents to produce useful answers. Datasets let you measure quality over time and feed evolution on Platform.
dataIntro.howBody:
1. New task in chat
   Summarize @README.md
   → file contents are embedded when the chain is generated and first run.

2. Re-run a saved chain
   /library → select a row → Run (or R on the inspection screen)
   → edit the task and toggle context files.
   Note: /run <id> only opens the inspector — use Library → Run to execute.

3. Evaluation & evolution
   /dataset add <chain_id> "input" --expected "output"
   /dataset run <chain_id>
   → grows the eval set for scoring and evolution.

evolutionIntro.title: About chain evolution
evolutionIntro.whatBody: Evolution automatically improves a saved agent chain on GigaEvo Platform. The engine proposes variants (prompts, step wiring) and scores each one on your evaluation dataset — like a small genetic search over chain designs.
evolutionIntro.whyBody: A first draft chain is rarely optimal. Evolution finds better variants without hand-editing every step. You keep a reproducible agent in Memory and let the platform explore improvements against measurable examples.
evolutionIntro.howTitle: How to run it in MAESTRO
evolutionIntro.howBody:
1. Generate a chain in chat and save it to the library — with the Save button (or via /library).
2. Open the library, highlight a row, and press E — Evolve with my data.
3. In the Set up evolution modal, set the JSONL path (input and expected columns), optional rubric and limits — Launch.
4. On the evolution screen, watch the run; Accept winner (key a) promotes the best variant back to Memory.

Tip: /evolution list shows all runs. Esc on the evolution screen stops the run on Platform.

evolutionLaunch.title: Launch evolution: {id}
evolutionLaunch.errorNoChain: Enter a base chain id to evolve.
